articles.title: Wise Group shares tank after U.S. bank charter rejected. What’s next for the fintech.
articles.description: Wise Group shares fell as much as 11% on Friday as the U.K.-headquartered, U.S.-listed fintech had its application for a national bank charter denied.

articles.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced-labor justification
articles.description: U.S. trading partners have rejected the forced-labor rationale behind Trump's new global tariffs, while most signaled plans to keep negotiating.

articles.title: Most FDA advisers say peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 should be made available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
articles.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

articles.title: The August stock-market slump is a myth — so why does Wall Street keep repeating it?
articles.description: More than 200 years of data show stocks typically gain in August, while market volatility sits well below average.

articles.title: S&P 500 flashes sell signals — options traders are bracing for wild swings in Apple, Meta and Microsoft
articles.description: Nervous investors set the stage for massive post-earnings moves in Wall Street’s biggest stocks.

articles.title: Yes, the AI stock selloff looks terrifying. But it might actually save the bull market.

articles.title: AMD’s rivalry with Nvidia is increasingly moving into a new realm
articles.description: The chip makers are best known for competing in the GPU business, but they’re stepping up their offerings in the attractive market for server CPUs.

articles.title: The stock market’s calm is cracking. Here’s how to prepare for an August shock.

articles.title: Trump rolls out a new wave of global tariffs. Here are the latest rates.

articles.title: ServiceNow’s stock falls as a new AI threat overshadows earnings beat
articles.description: While ServiceNow’s earnings beat proved that its AI momentum is picking up, the launch of OpenAI Presence reignited fears of enterprise software disruption

articles.title: These 16 states are offering tax-free shopping days as back-to-school costs soar to over $800 per student
articles.description: As inflation continues to squeeze household budgets, parents and students are on a mission to find back-to-school bargains.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
articles.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

articles.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
articles.description: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

articles.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008
articles.description: Import prices were up 0.3% for the month, as a drop in energy was more than offset by increases elsewhere.

articles.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
articles.description: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

articles.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
articles.description: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

articles.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls

articles.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
articles.description: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

articles.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
articles.description: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

articles.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era

articles.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
articles.description: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.

articles.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics

articles.title: U.S. job creation cools in June with payrolls growth of just 57,000; unemployment rate at 4.2%
articles.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

articles.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
